findings.issue: CLI Reference table and Quick Start omit implemented commands: `testforge web` (Web GUI), `testforge research`, and `testforge coverage`, all present in `src/testforge/cli.py` and `testforge --help`.
findings.fix: Add rows for `web`, `research`, and `coverage`; document `pip install "testforge[web]"` and `testforge web [--host/--port]` alongside the TUI section.

findings.issue: Claims to be the full CLI reference but has no sections for `coverage`, `research`, or `web`, while the installed CLI lists those commands.
findings.fix: Add sections mirroring Click options/help for `coverage`, `research`, and `web` (including `[web]` extra requirement).

findings.issue: States script generation includes 'HTTP API scripts, and shell scripts', but `testforge.scripts.generator.generate_scripts` only registers `playwright` (`src/testforge/scripts/generator.py`).
findings.fix: Change bullet to Playwright-only script generation, or implement HTTP/shell generators before claiming them.

findings.issue: Claims 'HAR traces per test run' as shipped; runner uses pytest/subprocess and log evidenceâ€”no automatic HAR capture in execution path (only `EvidenceCollector.capture_network` helper in `evidence.py`). README line 41 correctly says HAR is planned.
findings.fix: Align CHANGELOG with README: HAR as planned/plumbing only, or wire HAR into browser runs and then document.

findings.issue: Notes say 'Ollama local-inference support is coming in v0.2' but `OllamaAdapter` exists (`src/testforge/llm/ollama.py`, `create_adapter` for `ollama`), and configuration/README document `llm_provider: ollama`.
findings.fix: Remove or rewrite the v0.2 note to reflect current Ollama support; fix `ollama.py` error message that references non-existent `testforge[ollama]` extra in `pyproject.toml`.

findings.issue: SSH example imports `SSHConnector` and uses `key_path`, but the implementation is `SshConnector` in `ssh.py` with `NotImplementedError` and a different constructor surface (`host`, `user`, `port` only).
findings.fix: Rename to `SshConnector`, mark section as placeholder/not implemented, and match the real signature or defer docs until implemented.

findings.issue: Input table lists 'GitHub repo' as a generic inputs/ option; parsing only treats `github:owner/repo` or `gh:owner/repo` (`input/parser.py`), not a normal `https://github.com/...` URL.
findings.fix: Document the required `github:` / `gh:` prefix and a concrete `testforge analyze` example.

findings.issue: Pipeline ASCII diagram shows HTTP/Shell/SSH execution columns; default `testforge run` executes `*.py` scripts via pytest (`runner.py`), not connector-based HTTP/Shell/SSH tracks.
findings.fix: Clarify that connectors are for custom/extension use and the default CLI path is Playwright/pytest scripts.

findings.issue: Says TestForge uses calendar versioning (calver.org) while the project uses semantic-style `0.1.0` and a `v0.1.0` release link.
findings.fix: Switch wording to SemVer or remove the calver claim.

findings.issue: Release URL `https://github.com/songblaq/testforge/releases/tag/v0.1.0` may 404 if the tag was never published (not verified in this audit).
findings.fix: Publish the tag or link to PyPI/commit until releases exist.
